attacking_style: Long-range breakaway specialist who conserves energy while others chase, then finishes with a strong sprint. Shows willingness to attack on technical descents and transitions rather than pure climbing.
team_tactics: Notably independent operator who resists teammate cooperation to conserve energy. Prioritizes individual effort management over collaborative chase work, even causing team friction.
terrain_preference: Technical descents and transition zones where positioning instinct matters more than pure power. Struggles on steep sustained climbing gradients where pure climbers excel.
vulnerability: Cannot match pure climbers on steep ramps. Struggles to organize or contribute effectively in chase groups. Exposed when race requires collaborative effort rather than opportunistic solo moves.
key_patterns: Long breakaway wins requiring energy conservation over 20+ kilometers (2 wins from distance)
Refuses workload sharing to preserve sprint finish, even with teammates (2 instances)
Attacks on technical descents and transitions, not sustained climbs (2 observations)
Caught by GC contenders in final meters when they bridge late (Roglič 2x)
Strong positioning instinct on technical terrain outweighs pure climbing power
form_trajectory: positive: First WorldTour win in 2022 after developing pattern shows progression from 2018 victories, demonstrating consistent tactical approach pays off at highest level
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 15
2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 7
